events.title: AusAlert test date and time: emergency warning siren will blast over most phones in Australia on Monday. Here’s what to know
events.description: National emergency test alert will be sent to all mobile devices on 27 July, with domestic violence advocates advising how to turn off safe phones Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Virtually every phone in the country will blare an emergency siren at 2pm AEST on Monday 27 July as part of the first Australia-wide test of a new national warning system called AusAlert. Most phones, and some smartwatches and tablets, will receive a pop-up notification and an alert sound that cannot be silenced – even if your device is on “do not disturb” or silent mode. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AusAlert test date and time: emergency warning siren will blast over most phones in Australia on Monday. Here’s what to know

events.title: Climate change has intensified Europe's risk of drought, scientists say
events.description: Human-caused climate change has intensified Europe's drought by making extreme heat, rather than low rainfall, the main driver of exceptionally dry conditions, an international team of scientists said on Thursday, warning that a warmer atmosphere is sharply increasing drought and wildfire risks.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Climate change has intensified Europe's risk of drought, scientists say

events.title: Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre
events.description: A 600-square-meter exhibition features Hamas tunnel replicas, attack vehicles, and documents revealing Hezbollah and Iran's roles in the Oct. 7 assault.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre

events.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
events.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 59th Asean foreign minister’s meeting is being held – will also caution world leaders against placing a toll on the strait of Hormuz after both Iran and the US threatened to do amid the protracted conflict. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal

events.title: Five soldiers killed in southern Thailand checkpoint attack
events.description: The assault in Narathiwat comes a day after a car bombing elsewhere in the province wounded a police officer.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Five soldiers killed in southern Thailand checkpoint attack

events.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses
events.description: Russian attacks and drone strikes killed at least two children and injured dozens more across Ukraine over recent days while severely damaging three critical aid warehouses, UN agencies reported on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses

events.title: Wildfires ravage Spain, France and Italy, killing three firefighters
events.description: Wildfires swept across southern Europe on Wednesday, killing three firefighters and forcing thousands of people to evacuate as extreme heat and dry conditions fuelled blazes in Sicily, southwestern France and central Spain. Rescue teams battled dozens of fires amid warnings over increasingly severe weather events.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires ravage Spain, France and Italy, killing three firefighters

events.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak has killed over 1,000 people
events.description: In tonight's edition, the Ebola outbreak in the DRC has crossed a devastating threshold, with more than 1,000 lives lost. Also, in Kenya, human rights groups warn that political “goons” are increasingly being used to disrupt rallies ahead of elections next year. And in Senegal, four years after devastating floods threatened the famous Pink Lake’s unique ecosystem, the lake is slowly regaining its iconic colour.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Congo's Ebola outbreak has killed over 1,000 people

events.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia

events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia

events.title: Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz attacks
events.description: US President Donald Trump warned on Wednesday that the US would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Tehran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The threat came as Washington and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, regional missile alerts sounded and diplomatic efforts remained stalled.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz attacks

events.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus
events.description: The Security Council discussed the situation on the ground in Syria amid reports of an Israeli attack in occupied territory and of a multi-national oil pipeline to counter the closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz – still the focus of the ongoing US-Iran war. The meeting follows UN independent investigators’ findings that security conditions remain fragile in the south as António Guterres heads to Damascus for his first visit to the country as Secretary-General.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus

events.title: Toxic gas slows efforts to rescue 13 workers trapped in India hydropower tunnel
events.description: Authorities say 13 workers are trapped inside a collapsed hydropower tunnel in India

events.title: DR Congo: Ebola outbreak still expanding, WHO sees signs of stabilization
events.description: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN health teams and partners are still racing to contain the rapidly expanding Ebola outbreak, despite ongoing insecurity in eastern areas where the virus has taken hold. As of 19 July, the DRC Government has recorded 2,423 confirmed cases, including 967 deaths and 469 recoveries.

events.title: ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela
events.description: When a 12-story apartment building pancaked in La Guaira, Venezuela, following two powerful earthquakes late last month, panic ensued to save people injured or trapped under the rubble.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela

events.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
events.description: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife , Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia news live: Origin Energy says hackers accessed customers’ partial bank details; WA court dismisses Woodside case against activists
events.description: Follow the day’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Self-reported domestic violence incidents declining in NSW but deaths and hospitalisations fail to fall New data shows self-reported domestic violence incidents in New South Wales have declined, but the number of women and children murdered or hospitalised in domestic and family violence incidents over the past eight years has not significantly budged. The decline in self-reported violence is significant because it reflects underlying levels of violence in the community, not just what is reported to police. It suggests that, despite rising reports of domestic violence to police, domestic violence in the community is not increasing and may be trending down. There’s a strong perception that violence against women is rising, which is understandable given increases in police-recorded incidents, heightened media attention and greater public awareness. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia news live: Origin Energy says hackers accessed customers’ partial bank details; WA court dismisses Woodside case against activists

events.title: French lawmakers approve a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
events.description: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.

events.title: Drug gang threats force French police to put southern mayor under protection
events.description: The mayor of Alès in southern France was taken from his home at night by an elite police unit amid fears of an imminent attack by the country’s most dreaded drug gang, officials said Tuesday. Christophe Rivenq had previously received death threats linked to the DZ Mafia gang, which has grown out of its Marseille base to become one of France’s main security threats.

events.title: Iran war: Trump threatens bridges over Hormuz Strait attacks
events.description: US President Trump has threatened to destroy a "bridge or power plant" for each ship fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's foreign minister said any attack would "compel a powerful and decisive response."

events.title: Beloved artist stabbed, killed in 'random' beach town daytime attack as police arrest 19-year-old suspect

events.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
events.description: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – as it happened
events.description: This live blog is now closed. Sign up for US Breaking News email US gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 – almost a dollar more than a year ago. It comes as Brent Crude , the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92 a barrel . Continue reading...

events.title: Global hunger declines again but progress remains uneven across regions
events.description: Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.description: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires top military commander amid ongoing protests
events.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.

events.title: Zinedine Zidane to be officially appointed as France manager on Tuesday
events.description: A new era will begin on Tuesday for 'Les Bleus'. Zinedine Zidane is expected to be officially appointed as the head coach of the French national team on Tuesday. The 54-year-old coach will sign a four-year contract, until the 2030 World Cup. He will bring an end to the Didier Deschamps era, who has been in charge of Les Bleus since 2012.

events.title: 'Mykhailo Fedorov was fired for his successes in fighting corruption in the defense sector'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Daria Kaleniuk, Executive Director of the Anti-corruption Action Centre. Ukraine's wartime challenge is no longer only military, but institutional. Ms. Kaleniuk contends that battlefield success and democratic resilience are inseparable from deep reform of the defence establishment. While welcoming President Zelensky's replacement of the commander-in-chief with a younger, reform-oriented military leader, she argues that this represents only "half" of the necessary changes. The more consequential battle, in her view, concerns governance.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Mykhailo Fedorov was fired for his successes in fighting corruption in the defense sector'

events.title: 'Cockroach Janta Party' takes India by storm
events.description: Born from a satirical retort to a passing comment by India's top judge, the "Cockroach Janta Party" has grown into a full-fledged movement protesting the government's mishandling of the nationwide medical entrance examination in May. Despite a violent crackdown on a peaceful march heading to the Indian parliament in New Delhi on Monday, members of the movement have kept up their protest. FRANCE 24 explains how the CJP came to be.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'Cockroach Janta Party' takes India by storm

events.title: Will the war on Iran affect ASEAN’s efforts to tackle regional issues?

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: US hits Iran as Houthis claim strikes on Saudi tankers

events.title: Exclusive: In Syria's Kurdish-majority city Kobane, postwar integration efforts get under way
events.description: Northern Syria’s Kobane, or Ayn al-Arab, was a flashpoint city during the Kurdish fight against the Islamic State (IS) group nearly a decade ago. Today, it’s at the heart of the Syrian government’s bid to integrate the northern Kurdish majority region and their fighters. FRANCE 24’s Wassim Nasr had a rare opportunity to witness the integration process.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Exclusive: In Syria's Kurdish-majority city Kobane, postwar integration efforts get under way

events.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

events.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach historic nuclear energy deal
events.description: The deal is another sign of growing US-Saudi ties during US President Donald Trump's second term. The agreement, known as a 123 Agreement, would allow Saudi Arabia to build nuclear reactors using US technology.

events.title: US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
events.description: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.

events.title: An accident took his legs. He adapted his motorcycle and rides on in Pakistan
events.description: A young man in Pakistan’s capital lost both legs in a road accident five years ago, but he refused to let that define his future. Today, 28-year-old Hamza Ali delivers meals across Islamabad, riding a modified motorcycle fitted with hand-operated controls. Ali appeared cheerful under the scorching sun as he told The Associated Press that he had worked for foodpanda, a delivery platform, before the accident. Then a bus he was riding collided with a truck. “After that accident, the first six months of my life were extremely difficult,” he said. “Then I told myself, ‘Ali, you have to do something on your own.’” After nearly three years of job hunting in his hometown of Multan, where employers turned him away because of his disability, Ali moved to Islamabad in 2023 on a friend’s advice that the capital’s wider roads make it easier to ride a modified motorcycle. Using his savings, he adapted his bike and asked foodpanda to reactivate his rider account. The company asked one question:...
events.ticker_label: 💥 An accident took his legs. He adapted his motorcycle and rides on in Pakistan

In an unearthed interview, the One Nation leader said the advancements of women’s rights in the family law system over the decades had become “broken” because they favoured women and were now “suppressing the males all the time”. events.title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead
events.description: Daniel Siad, ⁠a modelling scout with ​ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been ​found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday. Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

The question: will this mineral wealth build peace and prosperity, or fund conflict and corruption? Follow live updates throughout the day below and go here for full coverage from across the UN.

The nomination also includes Pointe du Hoc, the German artillery battery at Longues-sur-Mer, Port Mulberry, and numerous military cemeteries and memorials. The UN cultural agency is set to male a decision between July 24 and 27.

events.title: Sex trafficking in fashion: 'Outrageous that French legal system hasn't done anything by now'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Dana Thomas, Paris-based journalist, specialising in fashion and culture, and author of "Deluxe: How Luxury Lost Its Luster". For decades, allegations of sexual exploitation in the fashion industry, spanning the globe, have resurfaced, often surrounding powerful figures whose deaths have complicated any semblance of accountability. In the wake of Daniel Siad's sudden death, Thomas offers a scathing indictment of the systemic failures within French law enforcement, the modelling industry, and the highest echelons of power.

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group

The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

His promise came a day after thousands of protesters, led by the social media-driven Cockroach Janta Party, demonstrated in New Delhi, sparking fresh clashes with police. The snowballing protests have become one of the biggest challenges to Modi since his re-election for a third term in office in 2024.

events.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
events.description: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue reading...

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
events.description: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the west to Dunedin city in the east – will be plunged into daytime darkness for 2 minutes and 51 seconds. Continue reading...

events.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
events.description: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica. Continue reading...

events.description: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.

events.title: Group of Indigenous Taiwanese row over 100 miles to recreate their ancestors' journey
events.description: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.

events.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
events.description: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

events.title: Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopens, 9 months after heist
events.description: The 17th-century Apollo Gallery reopened to the public without precious jewels or other objects previously on display.

events.title: Who could be the next UN Secretary-General?
events.description: For the first time in a decade, the United Nations will soon choose a new Secretary-General. DW takes a look at the six candidates, what they stand for and the challenges they and the UN will face.

events.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag released, ordered not to leave Turkey
events.description: Alican Uludag was freed Wednesday, a day after being taken back into custody on new charges. The arrest had come just two months after he was released pending trial in a separate case.

events.title: Germany: Merz replaces key figure following surrogacy row
events.description: Thorsten Frei, a close confidant of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is taking over the role as parliamentary group leader for the conservative CDU/CSU bloc. The position is a powerful one.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Merz replaces key figure following surrogacy row

events.title: Germany's growing interest in Africa: A win for both sides?
events.description: Germany is deepening its footprint across Africa, as Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul embarks on his third trip to the continent in 2026. His top-level talks will focus on security, skills development and investment.

events.title: Female coaches want more representation in African football
events.description: Female head coaches will be a rare sight at the Women’s African Cup of Nations. However, there are some promising signs.

events.title: Can India become the next semiconductor hub after Taiwan?
events.description: India is investing billions to build a domestic semiconductor industry as major industrial powers seek to diversify supply chains beyond Taiwan.

events.title: US trial date set for Venezuela's ousted president Maduro
events.description: Venezuela's captured president Nicolas Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores appeared in a US court today over drugs trafficking charges.

events.title: Remains of 22 more infants found at former mother and baby home
events.description: The remains of a further 22 infants have been found at the site of a former mother and baby home in Ireland, investigators have said.

events.title: UK considered suspending trade agreement with Israel
events.description: The UK considered pausing its trade agreement with Israel, having undertaken an impact assessment on suspension last summer.

events.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead at Paris home
events.description: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris.

events.title: Rebel Wilson cleared of defaming star of her film on Instagram
events.description: Rebel Wilson has been cleared of defaming another actress on social media.

events.title: FBI Director Patel returns looted cultural artifacts to Indonesian President Prabowo
events.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto has received a collection of repatriated cultural artifacts from FBI Director Kash Patel during a meeting in Jakarta

events.title: Vatican defends secrecy in sensitive abuse case involving ex-Jesuit artist Marko Rupnik
events.description: The Vatican has confirmed that a church trial against a famous ex-Jesuit priest accused of spiritual and sexual abuse of women is ongoing

events.title: Hitler’s birthplace in Austria reopens as a police station to deter Nazi pilgrimages
events.description: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Austria has been inaugurated as a police station

events.title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after a brazen heist, but no jewels on display
events.description: The Louvre's Apollo gallery is reopening to the public for the first time since last year's crown jewels heist

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.description: Ukraine is continuing its fight against Russia with new military leadership

events.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive prehistoric skeleton
events.description: More than 2,000 volunteers are helping Polish scientists restore a Mastodonsaurus skeleton

events.title: Two of Puerto Rico's top government officials to step down as corruption allegations persist
events.description: Puerto Rico’s governor says that her embattled chief of staff, Francisco Domenech, and deputy chief of staff, Itza García, are stepping down next month as they deny corruption allegations

events.title: Arkansas cop tragically dies saving fiancée's life just hours after proposing
events.description: An Arkansas police officer proposed to his fiancée hours before drowning in Elk River while rescuing her from the current during a canoeing trip with friends.

events.title: Border K9 sniffs out 99 pounds of prohibited meat, plants and monitor lizard in suitcase from Thailand
events.description: K9 Maisy discovered 50 pounds of raw meat, mangoes and 16 pounds of Bengal monitor lizard in luggage from Thailand, prompting a wildlife seizure.

events.title: Watch: Missouri suspect dives headfirst off bridge in wild bid to evade deputies
events.description: Video captures a Missouri man sprinting across a bridge and diving headfirst into Table Rock Lake after leading Stone County deputies on a chase.

events.title: Manhunt underway after masked gunman allegedly shoots NYC cab driver in face during overnight robbery
events.description: A masked gunman allegedly shot a 21-year-old livery cab driver in the face during an attempted robbery in Brooklyn before fleeing with cash, according to police.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Manhunt underway after masked gunman allegedly shoots NYC cab driver in face during overnight robbery

events.title: Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion
events.description: A manhunt is underway in Pima County after a woman was sexually assaulted during a home invasion about 30 miles from where Nancy Guthrie vanished.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion

events.title: ‘Hero' mom dies after using last strength to save 4-year-old son from Florida river
events.description: Nicole Grayson, 43, jumped into the St. Johns River at Ed Stone Park to save her 4-year-old son, using all her strength to keep him above water.

events.title: Trump flexes power in Arizona primaries as Democrats dodge far-left upset and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.
